title: SΔϕ-17 — Openness Stabilization and Sacred Marker Reversal
package_title: SΔϕ-17 — Openness Stabilization and Sacred Marker Reversal AI-Readable Package
creator: Sofience
type: AI-readable package / working paper / openness stabilization and sacred marker reversal audit module
core_claim: Sacred markers can stabilize openness by making relational closure or commitment-breaking costly. Sacred marker labels reverse into coercive closure when they replace explanation, bypass validation, raise re-entry cost, and suppress editability.

primary_formulae.close_path: ClosePath(p) iff Cost(p) -> infinity relative to alternatives
primary_formulae.openness_stable: Open(Omega_rel) stable iff exists C: Cost(Close(Omega_rel)|C) >> Cost(Open(Omega_rel))
primary_formulae.sacred_marker: Sacred := M such that Cost(break|M) -> infinity
primary_formulae.expanded_label_abuse: ExplanationGap + Sacred/Moral/Affective/Identity Label + ValidationBypass + ReentryCostIncrease + EditabilitySuppression + ClosurePressure
primary_formulae.love_label_abuse: 'love' label + bypass(real/phantom attractor discrimination) + sacrifice cost externalization

do_not_use_as: claim that sacred markers are always bad
claim that love truth good evil humanity are meaningless
